description: Single photo of gray leaf spot (Pyricularia grisea) on St. Augustine grass in Houston TX. Tests correct disease identification and species-appropriate fungicide recommendations.

photos.caption: St. Augustine leaf blades showing classic gray leaf spot lesions — small, gray-to-tan oblong spots with brown margins scattered across multiple blades, with some blades showing yellowing.

profile.weatherSummary: 90 F, very humid, wet weather pattern
profile.notes: Noticed small gray spots on the grass blades about a week ago. Spots have spread and some areas are thinning. Recently fertilized with high-nitrogen product.
profile.currentRoutine: Mow at 3.5 inches, irrigate 3x per week. Applied high-nitrogen fertilizer 2 weeks ago.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Close-up of St. Augustine leaf blades with multiple gray-to-tan oblong lesions with distinct brown borders — the hallmark of Pyricularia grisea gray leaf spot. Several blades show yellowing around lesion clusters. Disease pressure appears moderate to high given the widespread spotting.
